sections.content:
Clicking on the white block (node 14) at row=41, col=31 had no effect on the board - only the timer bar changed. This means clicking on the blocks themselves doesn't paint them.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics. So far I know:
1. SPACE splits the white block into purple (top) + white (bottom)
2. Clicking the left UI button (white) toggles the block in the gray box to white
3. Clicking the right UI button (purple) toggles the block in the gray box to purple
4. Clicking on the blocks themselves does nothing
Maybe the gray box is a "selected color indicator" and SPACE applies that color to something. Let me think about this differently:
- The gray box contains a block that changes color based on which UI button I click
- Maybe SPACE applies the color shown in the gray box to the bottom white block
Let me try: click the purple UI button (to select purple in the gray box), then press SPACE to apply it to the white block below.
Actually wait - I need to check what the current state is. Let me look at the segmentation to see if anything changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check what changed by comparing previous and current frames
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
nodes = {n['id']: n for n in seg}
print("Current nodes:")
for nid, n in sorted(nodes.items()):
    print(f"  {nid}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:2]}...")

# Check if clicking on the white block had any effect
if last_transition:
    before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
    after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
    print("\nDiff between before and after:")
    for nid in set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys())):
        bn = before_nodes.get(nid)
        an = after_nodes.get(nid)
        if bn is None:
            print(f"  New node {nid}: {an['color']}, {an['pixels']}px")
        elif an is None:
            print(f"  Removed node {nid}: {bn['color']}, {bn['pixels']}px")
        elif bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']:
            print(f"  Node {nid}: pixels {bn['pixels']} -> {an['pixels']}")
        elif bn['color'] != an['color']:
            print(f"  Node {nid}: color {bn['color']} -> {an['color']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
